reference: My mother-in-law had breast cancer two years ago. Last summer, she was diagnosed with large B-cell lymphoma and underwent chemotherapy and had her spleen removed. She just went for her check-up positron emission tomography scan, and they found a hot spot in her lower left lobe that is two inches big and numerous hot spots throughout both of her lungs. She is waiting for a biopsy appointment. Does this mean she has cancer in her lungs now?
text: My mother-in-law had breast cancer 2 years ago; last summer she was diagnosed with large B-cell lymphoma and underwent chemotherapy and had her screen. She just went for her checkup, positron emission tomography scan, and they found a hotspot in her lower left lobe that is 2 inches big and numerous hotspots throughout both of her lungs. She is waiting for a biopsy appointment. Does this mean she has cancer in her lungs now?
